id.notes: Sequential ID = 1 assigned correctly even on failed extraction

title.notes: Cannot validate — no page loaded (Akamai block / proxy blank page)

original_price.notes: Optional field. Cannot validate — no page loaded

url.notes: Product URL correctly passed through to output

remarks.notes: GENERIC BLOCKED — meaningful error message for blocked page

issues.problem: All proxy modes fail to load pages from paulaschoice.com in the browser-service environment
issues.details: Tested 3 proxy configurations: (1) residential proxy: blank page (source_len=39, empty title), (2) datacenter proxy: blank page (source_len=39, empty title), (3) no-proxy: Akamai Access Denied from edgesuite.net with reference IDs. The site uses Akamai Bot Manager which returns 'Access Denied' from edgesuite.net when no proxy is used. When Bright Data proxies are used, they return near-empty pages (39 chars). This may be a proxy credential issue, proxy routing issue, or the browser-service container IP being geo-blocked.
issues.suggested_fix: This is an environment/proxy issue, NOT a scraper code issue. The scraper code is well-structured. Possible fixes: (1) Verify Bright Data proxy credentials are valid and not expired, (2) Test proxy from a different geographic location, (3) Try adding country-code routing to proxy username (e.g., country-us), (4) The scraper already has --no-proxy flag; if Akamai blocks direct, the proxy must be working — debug why source_len=39 from proxy. The 39-char response could be a proxy auth failure page rather than a page load.

issues.details: The input_urls.json has only 1 URL (c15-super-booster/777.html). The --sample flag caps at 5 products, so only 1 was tested. More diverse sample URLs would allow better validation of extraction across different product types.
issues.suggested_fix: Generate more product URLs from category/listing pages to populate input_urls.json with 5-10 diverse products (different categories, price ranges, availability states).

notes: Well-structured scraper with comprehensive error handling
Proper Akamai warmup strategy (18s wait + cookie acceptance + block detection)
JSON-LD extraction is the correct approach per product_analysis.json
Good use of WebDriverWait polling for JS-rendered JSON-LD blocks
Image filtering excludes non-product images (brand.assets, emoji, flags, icon, navigation)
Availability mapping covers all schema.org variants
Diagnostic logging provides useful debug info on failure
Retry logic with cooldown for warmup failures
Block page detection (Akamai, Cloudflare, CAPTCHA, generic)
Proper proxy tier selection with --proxy-tier and --no-proxy flags
Output format is correct JSON with proper metadata
Remarks field used meaningfully for error states

proxy_connectivity.notes: The proxy returns a 39-char response which likely indicates a proxy authentication failure page, not the target site. The no-proxy path correctly identifies Akamai blocking. This is an infrastructure issue, not a scraper code issue.

feedback_for_writer.summary: Scraper code is excellent but cannot be validated because all proxy modes fail to load pages in the browser-service environment. The extraction logic, error handling, and architecture are all well-designed. The blocker is proxy/connectivity, not code.
feedback_for_writer.blocking_issue: Proxy returns 39-char response (likely auth failure page). Need to verify Bright Data credentials work from the browser-service container.
code_suggestions: DEBUG PROXY: The 39-char source_len from proxy likely means the proxy itself is returning an error page (e.g., 'Invalid credentials' or 'Zone not found'). Add diagnostic logging that prints the actual 39-char content on blank page detection to identify the exact proxy error.
Add proxy authentication check: before warmup, make a simple HTTP request to httpbin.org or similar through the proxy to verify credentials work.
Consider trying the proxy with SSL verification disabled (ssl_verify: false is in config but may not be passed to SeleniumBase).
The scraper architecture is solid — once proxy connectivity is resolved, extraction should work based on the product_analysis.json confirmation that JSON-LD has all needed fields.
feedback_for_writer.validation_note: Since no pages loaded, field extraction could NOT be validated against actual page content. The scraper code correctly implements all field mappings from product_analysis.json (JSON-LD paths: Product.name, Product.offers.price, Product.offers.priceCurrency, Product.offers.availability, Product.image, Product.description, Product.brand, Product.sku, Product.mpn, Product.aggregateRating, Product.review).
